_comment: Single provider-routing policy shared by promptcraft, execution-orchestrator, cascade-dispatch, dm-review, assess, and research. Keep task-fit decisions here so provider routing does not drift across plugin docs.

targets.rationale: Coding-only calibration: subscriptionProfiles[activeSubscriptionProfile] is the sole target. Codex Pro 20x uses 65/0/35 Codex/Claude/OpenRouter; switching to codex-5x uses 40/0/60. Claude is non-coding-only and therefore has zero coding share. Apply the target as routing pressure only among eligible chunks; security and required live-tool capabilities remain authoritative and their exclusions must be receipted. DeepSeek V4 usage is counted under OpenRouter.

chunkKind._comment_kinds: config/docs/mechanical-logic/logic/ui/integration are ROUTING buckets, not manifest kind enum values. The manifest kind enum stays {ui, logic, integration, config}; docs and mechanical-logic are refinements carried by the manifest `executor` field (derived from kind + estimatedComplexity per manifest-schema.md Executor Mapping), not first-class kinds. model-cascade.class_from_kind bridges only the manifest kinds (logic/config/docs/ui/integration); mechanical-logic routes via `executor: openrouter` set at prompt-generation time, so it needs no class_from_kind entry.

config.rationale: Docs, receipts, JSON/YAML/TOML, and pure-prose edits fit cheap large-context text models.

docs.rationale: Documentation generation and cleanup is text-heavy and usually low visual-risk.

mechanical-logic.rationale: Rename follow-through, test tables, seed data, and migration edits are bounded mechanical code changes.

logic.rationale: Complex service and handler logic uses Codex agentic coding with OpenRouter fallback.

ui.rationale: UI implementation and code review use Codex with browser evidence; Claude is reserved for non-coding work.

integration.rationale: Cross-chunk wiring and route verification use Codex, with OpenRouter fallbacks and no Claude coding rung.

pattern-recognition-specialist.rationale: Pattern checks use GLM-5.2, then DeepSeek V4 Pro through OpenRouter, before Codex fallback.

code-simplicity-reviewer.rationale: Simplification review uses the quality-first OpenRouter mechanical ladder before Codex fallback.

doc-sync-reviewer.rationale: Documentation consistency uses OpenRouter large-context models at any diff size.

test-coverage-reviewer.rationale: Coverage gap detection is read-heavy and uses the OpenRouter mechanical ladder.

openrouter-bulk-analyst.rationale: Bulk/large-context analysis is OpenRouter-primary whenever OPENROUTER_API_KEY is present, not only over a line-count threshold.

security-auditor.rationale: Security code review is Codex-native and never delegated to OpenRouter.

architecture-reviewer.rationale: Architecture code review is Codex-native; Claude is non-coding-only.

assessFanout.rationale: Assessment fan-out is read-heavy summarization; keep final synthesis on Claude.

researchFanout.rationale: Research fan-out is read-heavy summarization; keep final synthesis on Claude.

adversarialReview.rationale: Default adversarial review uses two non-Claude lenses; Claude plan-adversary is an optional third lens or tiebreak.

security._comment: Validated mirror of the OpenRouter-owned delegation policy. The canonical installed-runtime source is plugins/openrouter/skills/openrouter-delegate/references/delegation-security-policy.json. This block keeps pipeline planning self-contained and tools/validate-routing-economics.sh rejects drift.

neverRouteToOpenRouter.rationale: Auth, federation trust, secret encryption, destructive-confirmation tokens, email-delivery secrets, deploy secrets, and env files stay on Codex-native execution and substantive review. Mechanical review may delegate only the filtered safe remainder.

refusePatterns: migration or seed files containing credentials
contentRedaction.rationale: Strip or refuse sensitive content before delegation. If it remains after filtering, return the chunk to Codex-native review.

positiveRouting.rationale: These are the OpenRouter mechanical lanes when no security boundary is matched.
